markdown:
# Design Context

## Project

- Project name: 버티컬 슬라이스 아키텍처를 사용해야하는 이유
- Collection date: 2026-05-10
- Output purpose: 바이브코딩에서 AI가 기능 맥락을 참고하기 쉬운 구조로 버티컬 슬라이스를 설명하는 약 3분 한국어 기술 해설 영상
- Format: 1920x1080 horizontal
- Target viewer: AI 코딩 도구로 앱을 만드는 개발자, 1인 빌더, 팀 리드
- Viewing situation: YouTube desktop/mobile horizontal playback
- Primary content type: explain
- Secondary content types: tutorial, product
- Renderer targets: hyperframes

## Sources And Assets

- Official source links: not required
- Logo path: not required
- Product image path: not required
- UI screenshot path: not required
- Reference path: internal vertical-slide style and existing Hyperframes motion primitives

## Visual System

- Color tokens: near-black background, warm orange slice accent, cyan focus/flow accent, muted gray layers, white Korean text
- Font choices: Korean sans-serif, bold display for section claims
- Visual mood: focused technical board, clean architecture diagram, no decorative screenshots
- Motion personality: one active idea at a time, previous context remains dimmed
- Caption safe zone: bottom safe-zone text-only captions from ElevenLabs SRT, no progress bar
- Primary visual strategy: HTML/SVG/GSAP diagrams comparing layer folders, scattered feature context, and feature slices
- Motion grammar: technical-grid + product-demo
- Liquid Glass use: focus lens on selected slice or active module only
- Reference anti-patterns: empty glass panels, random dots, decorative connector lines, tiny unreadable captures, all elements visible from start

## Scene Visual Density

Use this table before Hyperframes implementation. It prevents large empty panels and makes imagegen decisions explicit.

| Scene | Scene Recipe | Primary Visual Route | Visual Density | Reveal Mode | Motion Layer | Imagegen Role | Renderer Targets | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 01 | product/before-after-demo | hyperframes | standard | one-at-a-time | layer labels, role badges | not_required | hyperframes | existing layer structure, calm and neutral |
| 02 | product/before-after-demo | hyperframes | standard | one-at-a-time | benefit card reveal | not_required | hyperframes | acknowledge role separation benefits |
| 03 | explain/mechanism-state-machine | hyperframes | rich | one-at-a-time | context path jumps, missed file warning | not_required | hyperframes | related feature files are scattered |
| 04 | explain/mechanism-state-machine | hyperframes | rich | one-at-a-time | module grouping, slice boundary lock | not_required | hyperframes | feature slice groups related modules |
| 05 | explain/fan-out-fan-in | hyperframes | rich | one-at-a-time | focus lens scans modules | not_required | hyperframes | AI follows feature context inside one slice |
| 06 | product/before-after-demo | hyperframes | standard | one-at-a-time | benefit lanes, before/after ticks | not_required | hyperframes | three benefits appear sequentially |
| 07 | explain/mechanism-state-machine | hyperframes | standard | one-at-a-time | prompt type-in, scope lock | not_required | hyperframes | prompt rule must be readable |
| 08 | explain/mechanism-state-machine | hyperframes | rich | one-at-a-time | shared base lock, feature slices rise | not_required | hyperframes | common stays shared, features vertical |
| 09 | visual-essay/imagegen-cinematic-sequence | hyperframes | standard | one-at-a-time | final architecture lock | support | hyperframes | imagegen support only for thumbnail |

## Scene Visual Thesis

No scene should reach render without a visual thesis.

| Scene | Visual Thesis | Viewer Must Understand | Primary Visual Mechanism | Narration Verb | Motion Must Show | Primary Asset | Failure If |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 01 | Layered structure is the familiar baseline. | The video is not saying old structures are simply wrong. | horizontal layer stack | divide | Controller/Service/Repository/Entity/DTO appear as roles | Hyperframes diagram | starts by attacking layers |
| 02 | Existing structures have real benefits. | Role separation helps humans and large teams. | benefit cards anchored to layers | clarify | benefits appear one at a time | Hyperframes diagram | no balanced setup |
| 03 | AI loses context when one feature is scattered. | A single feature can span many folders. | scattered file map | jump | context path jumps across folders | Hyperframes diagram | static file list only |
| 04 | Vertical slice groups one feature's flow. | Controller, service, repository, entity, DTO, and tests can sit near the feature. | module grouping into slice | group | related modules move into one boundary | Hyperframes diagram | definition only |
| 05 | A feature slice is easier for AI to inspect. | AI can follow the feature's input/process/output in one area. | focus lens scanning modules | inspect | lens moves through slice internals | Hyperframes diagram | cards only |
| 06 | The benefit is operational. | Short context, nearby files, narrow edits. | three benefit lanes | shorten/narrow | lanes activate sequentially | Hyperframes diagram | checklist all visible from start |
| 07 | Prompting should name the slice boundary. | Ask AI to work inside one feature slice. | prompt card with scope fence | limit | prompt locks inside boundary | Hyperframes diagram | prompt unreadable |
| 08 | Common layers still exist. | Shared auth/design/db config can remain shared. | shared base plus vertical slices | share/group | base remains, slices rise | Hyperframes diagram | implies everything goes into feature folders |
| 09 | Final rule is balanced. | Share common code, group features vertically, assign AI by slice. | final architecture lock | assign | whole structure locks | Hyperframes diagram | generic slogan |

## Scene Motion Blueprint

Root cause prevention: this project must not be implemented from `Motion Layer` labels alone. Each scene needs a concrete screen object, state transition, beat ladder, and review frame.

| Scene | Primary Screen Object | Supporting Objects | Initial State | Active State | End State | Motion Beat Ladder | Hold / Carryover Rule | Snapshot Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 01 | Neutral backend layer stack: `Controller`, `Service`, `Repository`, `Entity`, `DTO` | role badges, folder rail, small section label `기존 레이어 구조` | dark grid and title only | each layer becomes a solid horizontal band with its role label | full layer stack remains calm and readable | title -> layer bands appear one by one -> role badges attach -> stack holds | keep all layers visible into Scene 02 as neutral context; do not imply failure yet | all five layer names readable; no random lines, no crowded cards |
| 02 | Benefit board anchored to the existing layer stack | three benefit chips: `역할 분리`, `규칙`, `큰 팀`, subtle check marks | Scene 01 layer stack dimmed in background | one benefit chip expands at a time and attaches to the relevant layer | benefits compress to small badges, layer stack still visible | benefit 1 expands -> benefit 2 expands -> benefit 3 expands -> all compress | previous benefits stay dimmed; this scene must feel fair, not anti-layer | one active benefit dominates; old architecture is shown as useful |
| 03 | Scattered signup feature file map | file cards: `SignupController`, `SignupService`, `UserRepository`, `UserEntity`, `SignupDto`, `SignupTest`; AI cursor/lens; context trail | same layer stack breaks into separated folders | AI lens jumps folder to folder, context trail length grows, missed-file warning appears once | scattered map freezes with `컨텍스트 증가` warning | feature request appears -> files highlight in different folders -> lens jumps 4-5 times -> trail grows -> missed DTO/test warning | keep visited files faintly lit so viewer sees why the context became long | a single feature is visibly spread across multiple layer folders |
| 04 | `features/signup/` vertical slice boundary | grouped mini tree: controller, service, repository, entity, DTO, validation, test | scattered cards from Scene 03 remain visible | related cards move into one vertical slice container; unrelated common code stays outside | slice boundary locks with label `회원가입 슬라이스` | scattered cards gather -> folder tree forms -> boundary line closes -> lock tick | carry over one dimmed old-layer ghost behind it to show contrast | viewer can see the same files are now close together by feature |
| 05 | AI focus lens inside one feature slice | input/process/output rail, module chips, small `읽는 순서` indicator | slice from Scene 04 is visible but dimmed | focus lens travels controller -> service -> repository -> entity/DTO -> test; each step reveals one Korean note | a connected feature path remains highlighted | lens enters slice -> scans each module -> route rail connects modules -> final `맥락 한 번에` badge appears | keep scanned modules visible; outside features remain dimmed, not removed | the motion shows inspection within one bounded slice, not generic scanning |
| 06 | Three operational benefit lanes | lane 1 token budget bar, lane 2 file-distance ruler, lane 3 blast-radius ring | three lane titles only | each lane activates alone and shows a before/after metric | three compact result badges remain | context bar shrinks -> distance ruler shortens -> blast ring contracts -> all results align | only one lane is active at a time; previous results remain as small locked chips | viewer sees `짧아짐`, `가까워짐`, `좁아짐` as actual visual changes |
| 07 | Prompt editor with slice scope fence | bad prompt line, good prompt line, accepted file checklist | empty prompt editor with cursor | bad broad prompt appears then collapses; good slice-scoped prompt types in; scope fence locks around files | good prompt and scope checklist remain | bad prompt flash -> collapse -> good prompt type-in -> scope fence closes -> checklist ticks | do not keep the bad prompt dominant; final frame must teach the usable wording | good prompt is readable and clearly bounded to one feature slice |
| 08 | Shared core base plus vertical feature columns | shared base: `auth`, `design-system`, `db-config`; slices: `signup`, `upload`, `payment` | shared base appears first, feature columns hidden | base locks; feature columns rise above it; `공통은 공유` and `기능은 세로` labels appear sequentially | base and slices form one stable architecture | base appears -> lock marker -> each slice rises -> common/slice labels appear -> final distinction holds | shared base must stay visible to prevent the wrong takeaway that everything moves into feature folders | viewer sees shared infrastructure and vertical slices at the same time |
| 09 | Final architecture lock and AI task handoff | common base, three feature slices, AI task cursor, final phrase | architecture from Scene 08 remains dimmed | AI task cursor selects one slice; final rule appears in three beats | final phrase and selected slice lock | select common base -> select feature slice -> AI cursor lands on one slice -> final phrase locks | carry over architecture from Scene 08; end on clean rule, not more cards | final frame clearly says: common shared, features vertical, AI works by slice |

## Motion Implementation Contract

| Scene | Required DOM / Data Markers | Required Primitive | Timing Driver | Safe Zone Rule | QA Must See |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 01 | `data-scene="01"`, `data-layer-name`, `data-role-badge`, `data-progressive-disclosure="one-at-a-time"` | `layerReveal`, `badgeAttach` | scene local time from SRT cue group | layer stack above bottom caption zone | five backend layer labels appear sequentially and remain readable |
| 02 | `data-scene="02"`, `data-benefit`, `data-focus-item` | `sequentialFocus`, `stateBadge` | narration clauses for benefits | benefit text not behind captions | only current benefit is bright; previous benefits are dimmed context |
| 03 | `data-scene="03"`, `data-file-card`, `data-folder-zone`, `data-ai-lens`, `data-context-trail` | `contextJump`, `trailGrow`, `warningPulse` | cue group for scattered-context explanation | jumping lens never crosses caption bar | file cards are spread across folders and AI path visibly jumps |
| 04 | `data-scene="04"`, `data-slice-boundary`, `data-slice-file`, `data-boundary-lock` | `groupIntoSlice`, `boundaryLock` | definition cue group | slice tree stays above caption safe zone | same feature files gather inside one slice folder |
| 05 | `data-scene="05"`, `data-focus-lens`, `data-slice-module`, `data-route-rail` | `liquidLens`, `routeRail`, `scanFocus` | AI-fit cue group | lens and labels do not cover captions | inspection route stays inside one feature slice |
| 06 | `data-scene="06"`, `data-benefit-lane`, `data-before-after`, `data-metric-change` | `metricShrink`, `distanceShorten`, `blastRadiusContract` | three-benefit cue group | lanes leave bottom caption area empty | three benefits are visual changes, not just text cards |
| 07 | `data-scene="07"`, `data-prompt-line`, `data-scope-fence`, `data-check-item` | `typeIn`, `scopeLock`, `checkReveal` | prompt-rule cue group | prompt editor sits above captions | bad prompt collapses and good slice-scoped prompt remains readable |
| 08 | `data-scene="08"`, `data-shared-base`, `data-feature-slice`, `data-common-lock` | `baseLock`, `sliceRise`, `contrastLabel` | caveat cue group | shared base labels stay above captions | common/shared layer and vertical feature slices are visually distinct |
| 09 | `data-scene="09"`, `data-final-rule`, `data-ai-task-cursor`, `data-architecture-lock` | `taskAssign`, `phraseLock`, `finalHold` | payoff cue group | final phrase and captions do not overlap | final rule is readable without needing narration |

## Constraints

- Forbidden styles: source stamps, tiny captures, random teal dots, broad decorative lines, global glassmorphism cards, caption progress bars
- Unknowns: exact TTS duration until ElevenLabs generation
